title: corynephorus divaricatus subsp. articulatus
intro: Corynephorus divaricatus subsp. articulatus, also known as the forked hair-grass or forked tussock-grass, is a plant species in the genus Corynephorus. It is native to Europe and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestive health, and supporting immune function. It can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs in traditional medicine practices.
